findings.evidence: Lines 52, 67, 117, and 131 are `@[expose] def ...`; lines 59-63, 73-77, 124-127, and 136-139 already provide the public normal forms.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these four definitions; keep the existing simp restatement lemmas, adding any missing lemma instead of relying on reducibility.
findings.issue: `weightedAbelJacobiDegreeZeroDivisor`, `weightedAbelJacobiQuotientClass`, `unweightedAbelJacobiDegreeZeroDivisor`, and `unweightedAbelJacobiQuotientClass` are all marked `@[expose]`, exposing constructor bodies even though public `coe_*` and `*_mk` restatements are provided.

findings.evidence: New API grep hits only `weightedAbelJacobiQuotientClass_mk`, equivalence lemmas, and `_ofPoint_base` at lines 73, 82, 95, 105, 136, 144, 157, 167; the analogous existing API has `weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass_add` line 103, `zsmul` line 113, `eq_sum` line 121, `eq_iff_linearlyEquivalent` line 163, and `principalDivisor` line 218, plus unweighted versions at lines 282, 313, and 338.
findings.fix: Add the weighted and unweighted characteristic API analogous to `AbelJacobiSum`: `add`, `zsmul`, finite-sum, principal-divisor-zero, and equality/linear-equivalence criteria, with appropriate `[simp]` annotations on normal forms.
findings.issue: The public quotient-class maps are bare functions with only `mk`, equivalence, point, and base-point lemmas, so consumers cannot use the quotient Abel-Jacobi representative as an additive divisor sum without unfolding or transporting through Pic⁰.

summary: The new quotient representatives expose more implementation surface than needed and do not provide the characteristic additive/equality API needed to use them as Abel-Jacobi sums in the quotient model.
